description_long: This object consists of two glass bottles, one brown and one green, both with a classic long-necked beer or soda bottle shape. The bottles have a cylindrical body that tapers into a narrower neck, topped with a small, slightly raised rim that suggests a crown cap closure. The surfaces of both bottles are smooth and unadorned, with no visible labels, embossing, or decorative elements. The brown bottle appears slightly more matte, while the green bottle has a subtle sheen. Both bottles are empty, as no liquid or contents are visible through the glass. There are no visible signs of damage, wear, or deformation on either bottle.
description: Two smooth, long-necked glass bottles—one brown and one green—stand side by side, both unlabelled and undamaged.
description_view_0: The brown bottle is on the left and the green bottle on the right, both showing their side profiles with smooth, unmarked surfaces.
description_view_1: The green bottle is in the foreground, directly facing the viewer, partially obscuring the brown bottle behind it.
description_view_2: The green bottle is on the left and the brown bottle on the right, both angled to show their opposite sides compared to view 0.
description_view_3: The brown bottle is in the foreground, directly facing the viewer, partially obscuring the green bottle behind it.
